comment.body: Hi everyone. Wanted to share some open source code on trading view that I have found extremely helpful taking Alma's daily narrative and plotting it on the SPX chart. I then copy Alma's narrative and ask it to update the script each morning to only display the levels she provides that day. comment.body:
i take volatility into account when I code the regression. It is polinominal regression instead of linear that is used for SD channels (ToS has an inbuilt stdev channel script if i know correctly), 
and take volume and vol into account, in order to differ the difference momentums and their significance. 
then I use volume weighted moving avgs, three with different lenght, and Z-scores that it must hit. 
But none of them are round numbers. It took me a lot of backtest to find the best fit calibration
comment.raw_body:
i take volatility into account when I code the regression. It is polinominal regression instead of linear that is used for SD channels (ToS has an inbuilt stdev channel script if i know correctly), 
and take volume and vol into account, in order to differ the difference momentums and their significance. 
then I use volume weighted moving avgs, three with different lenght, and Z-scores that it must hit. 
But none of them are round numbers. It took me a lot of backtest to find the best fit calibration

comment.body:
I take a whack at it 

1.) in short, an event with a non zero probability 
2.) volatility is “real velocity” of price action which indirectly gives us a picture on liquidity, so this would show us the intensity of demand/supply
3.) if I am short speed my gamma is negative so if “put” gamma hurts downside and if “call” gamma hurts upside

comment.body:
no-no...
1) every event is non-zero prob event. Risk is the amplitude of price fluctuations.
2) volatility give us a pic about the liquidity inversely: higher vol = lower liquidity, and vice versa (the reason is explained in my educational post)
3) if you short upside gamma (otm call gamma), price is accelerant to the upside. gamma is inverse to vol, so vol increases, price fluctuations increases. Right tail risk

comment.body: I don't think Thursday's ATH was any kind of proper top.  Doesn't mean there won't be more scare for the bulls.  Tops tend to punish both bulls and bears with sharp moves, in my experience.  I don't think we've seen that yet.
